chunks.header: The Online Safety Act 2023 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2025
chunks.text:
Section heading: The Online Safety Act 2023 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2025

Made

17th July 2025

The Secretary of State makes these Regulations in exercise of the powers conferred by section 240(1) and (2) of the Online Safety Act 2023().
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: The Online Safety Act 2023 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2025

Made

17th July 2025

The Secretary of State makes these Regulations in exercise of the powers conferred by section 240(1) and (2) of the Online Safety Act 2023().

chunks.text:
Section heading: Citation and extent

1.—(1) These Regulations may be cited as the Online Safety Act 2023 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2025.

(2) These Regulations extend to England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: Citation and extent

1.—(1) These Regulations may be cited as the Online Safety Act 2023 (Commencement No. 6) Regulations 2025.

(2) These Regulations extend to England and Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.

chunks.header: Provisions coming into force on 25 th July 2025
chunks.text:
Section heading: Provisions coming into force on 25 th July 2025

2. Section 210 of the Online Safety Act 2023 (repeal of Part 4B of the Communications Act 2003()) comes into force on 25th July 2025.

Jones of Whitchurch

Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State

Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

17th July 2025
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: Provisions coming into force on 25 th July 2025

2. Section 210 of the Online Safety Act 2023 (repeal of Part 4B of the Communications Act 2003()) comes into force on 25th July 2025.

Jones of Whitchurch

Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State

Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

17th July 2025

chunks.text:
Section heading: Explanatory Note

(This note is not part of the Regulations)

These Regulations are the sixth commencement regulations made under the Online Safety Act 2023 (c. 50) (“ the 2023 Act ”). They bring into force section 210 of the 2023 Act, which repeals the video-sharing platform regulatory regime under Part 4B of the Communications Act 2003 (c. 21) (“ the 2003 Act ”). Services known as “ pre-existing Part 4B services ” (defined in paragraph 1 of Part 1 of Schedule 17 to the 2023 Act) have been regulated under both the 2003 Act and the 2023 Act since entering a transition period on 10th January 2024. Detailed transitional arrangements are provided for in Schedule 17 to the 2023 Act.

These Regulations mark the end of the transition period. From 25th July 2025, pre-existing Part 4B services will be regulated wholly under the 2023 Act and will be subject to each of the relevant duties of care specified in Part 3 of the 2023 Act.

A full impact assessment has been published in relation to the 2023 Act and copies can be obtained from the UK Government website at https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/online-safety-act-enactment-impact-assessment or from the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology at 100 Parliament Street, London, SW1A 2BQ, United Kingdom.
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: Explanatory Note

(This note is not part of the Regulations)

These Regulations are the sixth commencement regulations made under the Online Safety Act 2023 (c. 50) (“ the 2023 Act ”). They bring into force section 210 of the 2023 Act, which repeals the video-sharing platform regulatory regime under Part 4B of the Communications Act 2003 (c. 21) (“ the 2003 Act ”). Services known as “ pre-existing Part 4B services ” (defined in paragraph 1 of Part 1 of Schedule 17 to the 2023 Act) have been regulated under both the 2003 Act and the 2023 Act since entering a transition period on 10th January 2024. Detailed transitional arrangements are provided for in Schedule 17 to the 2023 Act.

These Regulations mark the end of the transition period. From 25th July 2025, pre-existing Part 4B services will be regulated wholly under the 2023 Act and will be subject to each of the relevant duties of care specified in Part 3 of the 2023 Act.

A full impact assessment has been published in relation to the 2023 Act and copies can be obtained from the UK Government website at https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/online-safety-act-enactment-impact-assessment or from the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology at 100 Parliament Street, London, SW1A 2BQ, United Kingdom.

chunks.header: NOTE AS TO EARLIER COMMENCEMENT Regulations (Part 1/4)
chunks.text:
Section heading: NOTE AS TO EARLIER COMMENCEMENT Regulations

(This note is not part of the Regulations)

The following provisions of the Online Safety Act 2023 have been brought into force, or are being brought into force, by commencement Regulations made before the date of these Regulations. On the date these Regulations are made, the commencement date of provisions marked \* has not been reached.

| Provision | Date of Commencement | S.I.
chunks.display_text:
Section heading: NOTE AS TO EARLIER COMMENCEMENT Regulations

(This note is not part of the Regulations)

The following provisions of the Online Safety Act 2023 have been brought into force, or are being brought into force, by commencement Regulations made before the date of these Regulations. On the date these Regulations are made, the commencement date of provisions marked \* has not been reached.

| Provision | Date of Commencement | S.I.

chunks.header: NOTE AS TO EARLIER COMMENCEMENT Regulations (Part 4/4)
chunks.text: January 2024 | 2023/1420 | | section 211 and Schedule 17 | 10th January 2024 | 2023/1420 | | section 213 | 10th January 2024 | 2023/1420 | | sections 215 to 218 | 10th January 2024 | 2023/1420 | | section 220 | 10th January 2024 | 2023/1420 | (1) 2023 c. 50. Section 240(3) of that Act prevents section 210 from being commenced before the end of the period of six months beginning with the date specified in regulations made under paragraph 8(1) of Schedule 3. Regulation 2 of S.I. 2024/571 specified that date as 2nd September 2024. (2) 2003 c. 21.
chunks.display_text: (1) 2023 c. 50. Section 240(3) of that Act prevents section 210 from being commenced before the end of the period of six months beginning with the date specified in regulations made under paragraph 8(1) of Schedule 3. Regulation 2 of S.I. 2024/571 specified that date as 2nd September 2024. (2) 2003 c. 21.
